source_pr.title: test: fix unstable TestIssue20658
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/24311 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: 

- The table structure is `create table t (a int, b int);`.
- The data contains 1000 records, each record is consist of 2 random number selected from `[0, 99]`.
- The query is `select /*+ stream_agg() */ samp_var(a) from t group by b;`

For the built-in function `samp_var()`, if there is only one element to aggregate, the result is `NULL`. The test `TestIssue20658` incorrectly assumes the result must be a float number.

### What is changed and how it works?

Allow the result to be "NULL".

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test

Side effects

NA

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to environment/external dependencies (resource contention, ports, or real cluster timing). This PR stabilizes it by: test: fix unstable TestIssue20658.
